subtopics.title: Livestock Farming
common_misconceptions: Animal farming is only for Fulani people
Free range is always better than intensive
subtopics.teaching_note: Livestock: cattle (beef, dairy, draught), poultry (chicken, turkey for meat and eggs), goats and sheep (meat, skin), pigs (pork), rabbits. Housing: protect from weather and predators. Feeding: balanced diet for growth and production. Disease control: vaccination, sanitation, deworming. Nigerian livestock: Fulani cattle (Boran, White Fulani), indigenous chicken, West African Dwarf goat. Challenges: pests (tsetse fly), diseases (bird flu, foot-and-mouth), inadequate veterinary services.
